title: erodium guttatum subsp. guttatum
intro: Erodium guttatum subsp. guttatum, also known as the spotted herb or redstem filaree, is a perennial plant belonging to the geranium family (Geraniaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The health benefits of Erodium guttatum subsp. guttatum include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
